---
title: "Pandemic Disruptions and Post-COVID Patterns of Antimicrobial Resistance-Associated Infections in Sweden"
subtitle: "Regional Incidence Patterns for ESBL/CARBA, VRE, and MRSA (2015-2025)"
author: ""
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{longtable}
  - \usepackage{array}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \floatplacement{figure}{H}
  - \floatplacement{table}{H}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.pos = "H"
)

# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(MASS)
library(broom)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(pxweb)
```

# Introduction

This analysis examines the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the epidemiology of three major antimicrobial resistance (AMR)-associated infections in Sweden: extended-spectrum beta-lactamase and carbapenemase-producing bacteria (ESBL/CARBA), vancomycin-resistant enterococci (VRE), and methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Using regional surveillance data from the Swedish Public Health Agency (Folkhälsomyndigheten), we compare incidence patterns across three distinct time periods:

- **Pre-COVID (2015-2019):** Baseline period before pandemic-related disruptions
- **COVID (2020-2021):** Period of pandemic restrictions including travel limitations and behavioral changes
- **Post-COVID (2022-2025):** Recovery period following relaxation of restrictions

We employ generalized linear models to estimate incidence rate ratios (IRRs) comparing these periods, providing insight into how pandemic-related disruptions affected the transmission dynamics of these AMR-associated infections.

Throughout, we frame the pandemic period as a **system-wide disruption** (affecting transmission, care-seeking, testing, and reporting) rather than a single causal exposure, and interpret changes as reflecting the combined influence of these mechanisms.

# Methods

## Data Sources

Annual regional case counts and incidence rates (per 100,000 population) for ESBL/CARBA, VRE, and MRSA were obtained from the Swedish Public Health Agency's national notifiable disease surveillance system (SmiNet) for the period 2015-2025 across all 21 Swedish counties. Population denominators were retrieved from Statistics Sweden (SCB) via the PxWeb API. For 2015-2024, annual county populations were obtained from SCB table `BefolkningNy` (contents `Folkmängd`) using **total age** and aggregated across sex and civil status to obtain total population. For 2025, annual population was not yet available in `BefolkningNy`; therefore, county population for 2025 was taken from SCB's monthly table `BefolkManadCKM` using the latest available month in 2025 at time of analysis and using **total age** and **both sexes combined**.

## Statistical Analysis

For each pathogen, we fitted negative binomial regression models with **case counts** as the outcome, including **log-population as an offset** to model incidence rates, and adjusting for **region (fixed effects)**. To account for within-county temporal dependence (repeated observations of the same county over time), all standard errors, 95% confidence intervals, and p-values were computed using **cluster-robust (sandwich) standard errors** clustered at the county level (HC1 variant; `sandwich::vcovCL`). The COVID period (Pre-COVID, COVID, Post-COVID) was the primary exposure variable. Incidence rate ratios (IRRs) with 95% confidence intervals were calculated comparing:

1. COVID period vs. Pre-COVID (reference)
2. Post-COVID period vs. Pre-COVID (reference)
3. Post-COVID period vs. COVID (reference)

As a secondary analysis, we fitted an **interrupted time-series (ITS)** negative binomial model to annual county-level case counts (with log-population offset and region fixed effects), parameterizing the COVID-era disruptions as changes in **level** and **slope** at two interruption points (2020 and 2022). We use this model to visualize fitted trajectories and a counterfactual "no interruptions" trajectory representing continuation of the pre-2020 trend.

### Model formulas

For the period-based regression, we modeled the expected case count \(\mu_{ij}\) in region \(i\) and year \(j\) as:

$$\log(\mu_{ij}) = \log(P_{ij}) + \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot \mathbb{1}(\text{period}_j=\text{COVID}) + \beta_2 \cdot \mathbb{1}(\text{period}_j=\text{PostCOVID}) + \gamma_i$$

Equivalently, as a log-incidence model:

$$\log\left(\frac{\mu_{ij}}{P_{ij}}\right) = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot \mathbb{1}(\text{period}_j=\text{COVID}) + \beta_2 \cdot \mathbb{1}(\text{period}_j=\text{PostCOVID}) + \gamma_i$$

Where:

- \(Y_{ij} \sim \text{NegBin}(\mu_{ij}, \theta)\) = case count in region \(i\) and year \(j\)
- \(P_{ij}\) = population in region \(i\) and year \(j\) (offset term)
- \(\gamma_i\) = region fixed effect
- \(\theta\) = dispersion parameter

Incidence rate ratios (IRRs) are obtained by exponentiating coefficients:

$$\text{IRR} = e^{\beta}$$

Statistical significance was assessed at \(\alpha = 0.05\).

For the ITS model, we parameterized time as \(t_j = \text{year}_j - 2015\), with step and slope terms at 2020 and 2022:

$$\text{covid\_step}_j=\mathbb{1}(\text{year}_j \ge 2020), \quad \text{covid\_slope}_j=\max(0, \text{year}_j - 2020)$$
$$\text{post\_step}_j=\mathbb{1}(\text{year}_j \ge 2022), \quad \text{post\_slope}_j=\max(0, \text{year}_j - 2022)$$

The ITS negative binomial model was:

$$\log(\mu_{ij}) = \log(P_{ij}) + \beta_0 + \beta_t t_j + \beta_{cs}\text{covid\_step}_j + \beta_{csl}\text{covid\_slope}_j + \beta_{ps}\text{post\_step}_j + \beta_{psl}\text{post\_slope}_j + \gamma_i$$

The "no interruptions" counterfactual shown in the figures corresponds to setting the interruption terms to zero (i.e., \(\text{covid\_step}_j=\text{covid\_slope}_j=\text{post\_step}_j=\text{post\_slope}_j=0\) for all \(j\)), yielding a continuation of the pre-2020 trend.

All analyses were performed in R version `r getRversion()`.

```{r load-data}
# nolint start
# Load the dataset
dat <- read_csv("AMR_Associated_Infections.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(year >= 2015, year <= 2025)

# Fetch SCB population by county and year (2015-2024, annual)
fetch_scb_population_county <- function(years = 2015:2024,
                                       cache_path = "../scb_population_county_2015_2024.csv") {
  if (file.exists(cache_path)) {
    cached <- read_csv(cache_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
    # If cached regions are already län codes, use as-is
    if ("scb_region" %in% names(cached) && all(nchar(as.character(cached$scb_region)) == 2)) {
      return(cached)
    }
    # Otherwise, fall through and rebuild (older cache used text labels)
  }

  url <- "https://api.scb.se/OV0104/v1/doris/sv/ssd/START/BE/BE0101/BE0101A/BefolkningNy"
  md <- tryCatch(
    pxweb::pxweb_get(url),
    error = function(e) stop("SCB API call failed for annual population table: ", e$message)
  )

  # Dimension metadata
  region_var <- md[["variables"]][[1]]
  civil_var  <- md[["variables"]][[2]]
  age_var    <- md[["variables"]][[3]]
  sex_var    <- md[["variables"]][[4]]
  cont_var   <- md[["variables"]][[5]]
  tid_var    <- md[["variables"]][[6]]

  # County regions are the 21 two-digit län codes (exclude "00" = Riket)
  county_region_codes <- region_var$values[nchar(region_var$values) == 2 & region_var$values != "00"]

  # Safety: should be 21 counties
  if (length(county_region_codes) != 21) {
    stop("SCB query setup error: expected 21 county regions, found ", length(county_region_codes), ".")
  }

  years_chr <- as.character(years)
  years_chr <- years_chr[years_chr %in% tid_var$values]

  if (length(years_chr) == 0) {
    stop("SCB query setup error: none of the requested years exist in the SCB table.")
  }

  q <- pxweb::pxweb_query(list(
    Region = county_region_codes,
    Civilstand = civil_var$values,
    # IMPORTANT: use total age only to avoid double-counting (table includes both Totalt and single ages)
    Alder = if ("tot" %in% age_var$values) "tot" else age_var$values[grepl("^tot", tolower(age_var$valueTexts))][1],
    Kon = sex_var$values,
    ContentsCode = "BE0101N1",  # Folkmängd
    Tid = years_chr
  ))

  # pxweb_get_data may return either a pxweb object or a data.frame depending on pxweb version
  res <- tryCatch(
    pxweb::pxweb_get_data(url, q),
    error = function(e) stop("SCB API call failed to fetch population data: ", e$message)
  )
  df <- if (is.data.frame(res)) res else pxweb::pxweb_as_data_frame(res, column.name.type = "code", variable.value.type = "code")

  # Standardize key column names defensively
  region_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    "Region" %in% names(df) ~ "Region",
    "region" %in% names(df) ~ "region",
    TRUE ~ names(df)[1]
  )
  year_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    "Tid" %in% names(df) ~ "Tid",
    "år" %in% names(df) ~ "år",
    "year" %in% names(df) ~ "year",
    TRUE ~ names(df)[length(names(df)) - 1]
  )
  value_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    "value" %in% names(df) ~ "value",
    "values" %in% names(df) ~ "values",
    TRUE ~ names(df)[length(names(df))]
  )

  pop <- df %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      scb_region = !!region_col,
      year = !!year_col,
      value = !!value_col
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      year = as.integer(.data$year),
      value = as.numeric(.data$value),
      scb_region = as.character(.data$scb_region)
    ) %>%
    # If Region came back as text labels, map to SCB codes using metadata
    left_join(
      tibble::tibble(code = region_var$values, label = region_var$valueTexts),
      by = c("scb_region" = "label")
    ) %>%
    mutate(scb_region = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(.data$code), .data$code, .data$scb_region)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-code) %>%
    group_by(scb_region, year) %>%
    summarise(population = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  write_csv(pop, cache_path)
  pop
}

region_key <- tibble::tribble(
  ~region, ~scb_region,
  "Stockholm", "01",
  "Uppsala", "03",
  "Södermanland", "04",
  "Östergötland", "05",
  "Jönköping", "06",
  "Kronoberg", "07",
  "Kalmar", "08",
  "Gotland", "09",
  "Blekinge", "10",
  "Skåne", "12",
  "Halland", "13",
  "Västra Götaland", "14",
  "Värmland", "17",
  "Orebro", "18",
  "Västmanland", "19",
  "Dalarna", "20",
  "Gävleborg", "21",
  "Västernorrland", "22",
  "Jämtland", "23",
  "Västerbotten", "24",
  "Norrbotten", "25"
)

pop_2015_2024 <- fetch_scb_population_county(2015:2024)

# Check if 2025 is available in the annual table, otherwise use monthly table
url_annual <- "https://api.scb.se/OV0104/v1/doris/sv/ssd/START/BE/BE0101/BE0101A/BefolkningNy"
md_annual <- tryCatch(
  pxweb::pxweb_get(url_annual),
  error = function(e) stop("SCB API call failed to check 2025 availability: ", e$message)
)
tid_var_annual <- md_annual[["variables"]][[6]]
has_2025_annual <- "2025" %in% tid_var_annual$values

if (has_2025_annual) {
  # Use annual table for 2025
  pop_2025 <- fetch_scb_population_county(2025, cache_path = "../scb_population_county_2025.csv")
} else {
  # Fall back to monthly table for 2025
  # Fetch SCB population for 2025 from monthly table (latest available month)
  fetch_scb_population_2025_latest_month <- function(cache_prefix = "../scb_population_county_2025_") {
  url <- "https://api.scb.se/OV0104/v1/doris/sv/ssd/START/BE/BE0101/BE0101A/BefolkManadCKM"
  md <- tryCatch(
    pxweb::pxweb_get(url),
    error = function(e) stop("SCB API call failed for monthly population table: ", e$message)
  )
  vars <- md[["variables"]]
  codes <- vapply(vars, function(v) v[["code"]], character(1))

  region_var <- vars[[which(codes == "Region")]]
  age_var <- vars[[which(codes == "Alder")]]
  sex_var <- vars[[which(codes == "Kon")]]
  cont_var <- vars[[which(codes == "ContentsCode")]]
  tid_var <- vars[[which(codes == "Tid")]]

  # County regions are the 21 two-digit län codes (exclude "00" = Riket)
  county_region_codes <- region_var$values[nchar(region_var$values) == 2 & region_var$values != "00"]
  if (length(county_region_codes) != 21) {
    stop("SCB monthly query setup error: expected 21 county regions, found ", length(county_region_codes), ".")
  }

  # Pick the latest available month in 2025 (e.g., 2025M10)
  tid_2025 <- tid_var$values[grepl("^2025M", tid_var$values)]
  if (length(tid_2025) == 0) {
    stop("SCB monthly table does not contain any 2025 months (Tid starting with '2025M').")
  }
  latest_tid <- max(tid_2025)

  cache_path <- paste0(cache_prefix, latest_tid, ".csv")
  if (file.exists(cache_path)) {
    cached <- read_csv(cache_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
    if ("scb_region" %in% names(cached) && all(nchar(as.character(cached$scb_region)) == 2)) {
      return(cached)
    }
    # Otherwise, rebuild (older cache used text labels)
  }

  q <- pxweb::pxweb_query(list(
    Region = county_region_codes,
    # IMPORTANT: monthly table includes many overlapping age groups; query totals only
    Alder = if ("TotSA" %in% age_var$values) "TotSA" else age_var$values[1],
    Kon = if ("TotSa" %in% sex_var$values) "TotSa" else sex_var$values[1],
    ContentsCode = cont_var$values[1],
    Tid = latest_tid
  ))

  res <- tryCatch(
    pxweb::pxweb_get_data(url, q),
    error = function(e) stop("SCB API call failed to fetch monthly population data: ", e$message)
  )
  df <- if (is.data.frame(res)) res else pxweb::pxweb_as_data_frame(res, column.name.type = "code", variable.value.type = "code")

  # Standardize key column names defensively
  region_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    "Region" %in% names(df) ~ "Region",
    "region" %in% names(df) ~ "region",
    TRUE ~ names(df)[1]
  )
  value_col <- dplyr::case_when(
    "value" %in% names(df) ~ "value",
    "values" %in% names(df) ~ "values",
    TRUE ~ names(df)[length(names(df))]
  )

  pop_2025 <- df %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      scb_region = !!region_col,
      value = !!value_col
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      value = as.numeric(.data$value),
      scb_region = as.character(.data$scb_region)
    ) %>%
    # If Region came back as text labels, map to SCB codes using metadata
    left_join(
      tibble::tibble(code = region_var$values, label = region_var$valueTexts),
      by = c("scb_region" = "label")
    ) %>%
    mutate(scb_region = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(.data$code), .data$code, .data$scb_region)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-code) %>%
    group_by(scb_region) %>%
    summarise(population = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(year = 2025L)

  write_csv(pop_2025, cache_path)
  pop_2025
  }
  pop_2025 <- fetch_scb_population_2025_latest_month()
}

pop_all_years <- bind_rows(pop_2015_2024, pop_2025)

# Reshape case + incidence data to long format
dat_long <- dat %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(esbl_carba_cases, vre_cases, mrsa_cases),
    names_to = "pathogen",
    values_to = "cases"
  ) %>%
  mutate(    pathogen = case_when(
    pathogen == "esbl_carba_cases" ~ "ESBL/CARBA",
    pathogen == "vre_cases" ~ "VRE",
    pathogen == "mrsa_cases" ~ "MRSA"
  )) %>%
  left_join(
    dat %>%
      pivot_longer(
        cols = c(esbl_carba_incidence, vre_incidence, mrsa_incidence),
        names_to = "pathogen",
        values_to = "incidence"
      ) %>%
      mutate(pathogen = case_when(
        pathogen == "esbl_carba_incidence" ~ "ESBL/CARBA",
        pathogen == "vre_incidence" ~ "VRE",
        pathogen == "mrsa_incidence" ~ "MRSA"
      )) %>%
      dplyr::select(region, year, pathogen, incidence),
    by = c("region", "year", "pathogen")
  ) %>%
  left_join(region_key, by = "region") %>%
  left_join(pop_all_years, by = c("scb_region", "year")) %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      year <= 2019 ~ "Pre-COVID",
      year %in% 2020:2021 ~ "COVID",
      year >= 2022 ~ "Post-COVID"
    ),
    period = factor(period, levels = c("Pre-COVID", "COVID", "Post-COVID")),
    region = factor(region)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(population), population > 0)

# Fail fast if any county-year is missing population after join
missing_pop <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(region, year) %>%
  summarise(pop_ok = all(!is.na(population)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(!pop_ok)

if (nrow(missing_pop) > 0) {
  stop("Missing SCB population after join for: ", paste0(missing_pop$region, " ", missing_pop$year, collapse = ", "))
}
# nolint end
```

# Results

## Descriptive Statistics

### Overall Case Counts by Period

```{r summary-table}
# Summary statistics by pathogen and period
# First compute national annual totals, then summarize by period
national_annual <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(pathogen, period, year) %>%
  summarise(
    annual_cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    annual_incidence = weighted.mean(incidence, population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_stats <- national_annual %>%
  group_by(pathogen, period) %>%
  summarise(
    `Total Cases` = sum(annual_cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Mean Annual Cases` = round(mean(annual_cases, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `SD` = round(sd(annual_cases, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Mean Incidence` = round(mean(annual_incidence, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_stats %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Summary of AMR-associated infection cases and incidence rates by pathogen and COVID period",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")
```

### National Totals by Year

```{r national-trends}
national_trends <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(pathogen, year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_incidence = round(mean(incidence, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      year <= 2019 ~ "Pre-COVID",
      year %in% 2020:2021 ~ "COVID",
      year >= 2022 ~ "Post-COVID"
    )
  )

national_trends %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = year,
    names_from = pathogen,
    values_from = c(total_cases, mean_incidence),
    names_glue = "{pathogen}_{.value}"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Year = year,
    `ESBL/CARBA Cases` = `ESBL/CARBA_total_cases`,
    `ESBL/CARBA Inc.` = `ESBL/CARBA_mean_incidence`,
    `VRE Cases` = VRE_total_cases,
    `VRE Inc.` = VRE_mean_incidence,
    `MRSA Cases` = MRSA_total_cases,
    `MRSA Inc.` = MRSA_mean_incidence
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "National case totals and mean regional incidence rates by year",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("c", rep("r", 6))
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size = 9
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "ESBL/CARBA" = 2, "VRE" = 2, "MRSA" = 2))
```

## Regression Analysis

### Model Specification

Model equations and definitions are provided in the Methods section (Statistical Analysis).

### Incidence Rate Ratios

We fitted log-linear regression models for each pathogen to estimate the change in incidence rates between periods, adjusting for regional variation.

```{r regression-models}
# nolint start
# Function to fit Negative Binomial model with population offset, adjusted for region
fit_irr_model <- function(data, pathogen_name, ref_period = "Pre-COVID") {
  
  pathogen_data <- data %>%
    filter(pathogen == pathogen_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(cases), cases >= 0, !is.na(population), population > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      period = relevel(period, ref = ref_period),
      log_pop = log(population)
    )
  
  model <- MASS::glm.nb(cases ~ period + region + offset(log_pop), data = pathogen_data)

  # Cluster-robust SEs (clustered by region) via sandwich::vcovCL
  vcov_cl <- sandwich::vcovCL(model, cluster = pathogen_data$region, type = "HC1")
  ct <- lmtest::coeftest(model, vcov. = vcov_cl)

  coefs <- tibble::as_tibble(
    data.frame(
      term = rownames(ct),
      estimate = ct[, "Estimate"],
      std.error = ct[, "Std. Error"],
      p.value = ct[, "Pr(>|z|)"],
      row.names = NULL
    )
  ) %>%
    filter(str_detect(term, "period")) %>%
    mutate(
      IRR = exp(estimate),
      IRR_lower = exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),
      IRR_upper = exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error),
      comparison = case_when(
        term == "periodCOVID" & ref_period == "Pre-COVID" ~ "COVID vs Pre-COVID",
        term == "periodPost-COVID" & ref_period == "Pre-COVID" ~ "Post-COVID vs Pre-COVID",
        term == "periodPre-COVID" & ref_period == "COVID" ~ "Pre-COVID vs COVID",
        term == "periodPost-COVID" & ref_period == "COVID" ~ "Post-COVID vs COVID"
      ),
      pathogen = pathogen_name
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(comparison)) %>%
    dplyr::select(pathogen, comparison, IRR, IRR_lower, IRR_upper, p.value)
  
  return(coefs)
}

# Fit models for each pathogen with Pre-COVID as reference
irr_results <- bind_rows(
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "ESBL/CARBA", "Pre-COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "VRE", "Pre-COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "MRSA", "Pre-COVID")
)

# Also calculate Post-COVID vs COVID
irr_covid_ref <- bind_rows(
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "ESBL/CARBA", "COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "VRE", "COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long, "MRSA", "COVID")
) %>%
  filter(comparison == "Post-COVID vs COVID")

# Combine all results
all_irr <- bind_rows(irr_results, irr_covid_ref) %>%
  arrange(pathogen, comparison)
# nolint end
```

```{r irr-table}
all_irr %>%
  mutate(
    IRR_formatted = sprintf("%.2f", IRR),
    CI_formatted = sprintf("(%.2f - %.2f)", IRR_lower, IRR_upper),
    p_formatted = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Pathogen = pathogen,
    Comparison = comparison,
    IRR = IRR_formatted,
    `95% CI` = CI_formatted,
    `P-value` = p_formatted
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Incidence Rate Ratios (IRRs) comparing COVID periods, adjusted for region (cluster-robust SEs)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "c", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle") %>%
  footnote(
    general = "IRR < 1 indicates lower incidence compared to reference period; IRR > 1 indicates higher incidence.",
    general_title = "Note: ",
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

## Interrupted time-series (ITS) model (secondary analysis)

To better align the model with the study question (a disruption during 2020-2021 and a potential rebound after 2022) and with temporal patterns, we additionally fitted an **interrupted time-series (ITS)** negative binomial model with:

- A baseline time trend (year),
- A COVID interruption at 2020 (step + slope change),
- A post-COVID interruption at 2022 (step + slope change),
- Region fixed effects and a population offset.

```{r its-models}
# nolint start
dat_its <- dat_long %>%
  mutate(
    log_pop = log(population),
    t = year - 2015,
    covid_step = as.integer(year >= 2020),
    covid_slope = pmax(0, year - 2020),
    post_step = as.integer(year >= 2022),
    post_slope = pmax(0, year - 2022)
  )

fit_its <- function(data, pathogen_name) {
  d <- data %>% filter(pathogen == pathogen_name)

  m <- MASS::glm.nb(
    cases ~ t + covid_step + covid_slope + post_step + post_slope + region + offset(log_pop),
    data = d
  )

  # Cluster-robust SEs (clustered by region) via sandwich::vcovCL
  vcov_cl <- sandwich::vcovCL(m, cluster = d$region, type = "HC1")
  ct <- lmtest::coeftest(m, vcov. = vcov_cl)

  eff <- tibble::as_tibble(
    data.frame(
      term = rownames(ct),
      estimate = ct[, "Estimate"],
      std.error = ct[, "Std. Error"],
      p.value = ct[, "Pr(>|z|)"],
      row.names = NULL
    )
  ) %>%
    filter(term %in% c("covid_step", "covid_slope", "post_step", "post_slope")) %>%
    mutate(
      IRR = exp(estimate),
      IRR_lower = exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),
      IRR_upper = exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error),
      effect = dplyr::recode(
        term,
        covid_step = "COVID step (from 2020)",
        covid_slope = "COVID slope change (per year)",
        post_step = "Post step (from 2022)",
        post_slope = "Post slope change (per year)"
      ),
      pathogen = pathogen_name
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(pathogen, effect, IRR, IRR_lower, IRR_upper, p.value)

  list(model = m, effects = eff)
}

fit_period <- function(data, pathogen_name) {
  d <- data %>%
    filter(pathogen == pathogen_name) %>%
    mutate(log_pop = log(population))

  MASS::glm.nb(cases ~ period + region + offset(log_pop), data = d)
}

its_fits <- list(
  `ESBL/CARBA` = fit_its(dat_its, "ESBL/CARBA"),
  VRE = fit_its(dat_its, "VRE"),
  MRSA = fit_its(dat_its, "MRSA")
)

its_effects <- bind_rows(
  its_fits$`ESBL/CARBA`$effects,
  its_fits$VRE$effects,
  its_fits$MRSA$effects
)

aic_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  Pathogen = c("ESBL/CARBA", "VRE", "MRSA"),
  `AIC (Period model)` = c(
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long, "ESBL/CARBA")),
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long, "VRE")),
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long, "MRSA"))
  ),
  `AIC (ITS model)` = c(
    AIC(its_fits$`ESBL/CARBA`$model),
    AIC(its_fits$VRE$model),
    AIC(its_fits$MRSA$model)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    `AIC (Period model)` = round(`AIC (Period model)`, 1),
    `AIC (ITS model)` = round(`AIC (ITS model)`, 1)
  )
# nolint end
```

```{r its-table}
its_effects %>%
  mutate(
    IRR_formatted = sprintf("%.2f", IRR),
    CI_formatted = sprintf("(%.2f - %.2f)", IRR_lower, IRR_upper),
    p_formatted = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Pathogen = pathogen,
    Effect = effect,
    IRR = IRR_formatted,
    `95% CI` = CI_formatted,
    `P-value` = p_formatted
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Interrupted time-series effects (negative binomial + offset + region FE, cluster-robust SEs)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "c", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")
```

```{r its-aic-table}
aic_tbl %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Model fit comparison by AIC (lower is better)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  )
```

## Sensitivity analysis: excluding 2025

Because the 2025 population denominator is derived from the latest available SCB monthly table (not a finalized annual total), we re-run both the period model and the ITS model on the 2015-2024 data only to assess the robustness of results.

```{r sensitivity-data}
dat_long_no2025 <- dat_long %>% filter(year <= 2024)
```

### Period model (2015-2024)

```{r sensitivity-period-model}
# nolint start
irr_results_no2025 <- bind_rows(
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "ESBL/CARBA", "Pre-COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "VRE", "Pre-COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "MRSA", "Pre-COVID")
)

irr_covid_ref_no2025 <- bind_rows(
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "ESBL/CARBA", "COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "VRE", "COVID"),
  fit_irr_model(dat_long_no2025, "MRSA", "COVID")
) %>%
  filter(comparison == "Post-COVID vs COVID")

all_irr_no2025 <- bind_rows(irr_results_no2025, irr_covid_ref_no2025) %>%
  arrange(pathogen, comparison)
# nolint end
```

```{r sensitivity-irr-table}
all_irr_no2025 %>%
  mutate(
    IRR_formatted = sprintf("%.2f", IRR),
    CI_formatted = sprintf("(%.2f - %.2f)", IRR_lower, IRR_upper),
    p_formatted = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Pathogen = pathogen,
    Comparison = comparison,
    IRR = IRR_formatted,
    `95% CI` = CI_formatted,
    `P-value` = p_formatted
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Sensitivity: IRRs excluding 2025 (2015-2024 only), adjusted for region (cluster-robust SEs)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "c", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")
```

### ITS model (2015-2024)

```{r sensitivity-its-model}
# nolint start
dat_its_no2025 <- dat_long_no2025 %>%
  mutate(
    log_pop = log(population),
    t = year - 2015,
    covid_step = as.integer(year >= 2020),
    covid_slope = pmax(0, year - 2020),
    post_step = as.integer(year >= 2022),
    post_slope = pmax(0, year - 2022)
  )

its_fits_no2025 <- list(
  `ESBL/CARBA` = fit_its(dat_its_no2025, "ESBL/CARBA"),
  VRE = fit_its(dat_its_no2025, "VRE"),
  MRSA = fit_its(dat_its_no2025, "MRSA")
)

its_effects_no2025 <- bind_rows(
  its_fits_no2025$`ESBL/CARBA`$effects,
  its_fits_no2025$VRE$effects,
  its_fits_no2025$MRSA$effects
)

aic_tbl_no2025 <- tibble::tibble(
  Pathogen = c("ESBL/CARBA", "VRE", "MRSA"),
  `AIC (Period model)` = c(
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long_no2025, "ESBL/CARBA")),
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long_no2025, "VRE")),
    AIC(fit_period(dat_long_no2025, "MRSA"))
  ),
  `AIC (ITS model)` = c(
    AIC(its_fits_no2025$`ESBL/CARBA`$model),
    AIC(its_fits_no2025$VRE$model),
    AIC(its_fits_no2025$MRSA$model)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    `AIC (Period model)` = round(`AIC (Period model)`, 1),
    `AIC (ITS model)` = round(`AIC (ITS model)`, 1)
  )
# nolint end
```

```{r sensitivity-its-table}
its_effects_no2025 %>%
  mutate(
    IRR_formatted = sprintf("%.2f", IRR),
    CI_formatted = sprintf("(%.2f - %.2f)", IRR_lower, IRR_upper),
    p_formatted = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Pathogen = pathogen,
    Effect = effect,
    IRR = IRR_formatted,
    `95% CI` = CI_formatted,
    `P-value` = p_formatted
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Sensitivity: ITS effects excluding 2025 (2015-2024 only, cluster-robust SEs)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "c", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")
```

```{r sensitivity-aic-table}
aic_tbl_no2025 %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Sensitivity: AIC comparison excluding 2025 (lower is better)",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  )
```

## Counterfactual trajectories (ITS)

The following figures show national observed incidence versus the fitted ITS trajectory, alongside a counterfactual trajectory. Although the ITS model is fitted to county-level counts (with region fixed effects and a population offset), the plotted trajectories are shown at the national level by aggregating observed and model-predicted county counts and converting to incidence using the corresponding national population.

- **No interruptions:** continuation of the pre-2020 trend.

```{r its-counterfactual-esbl-carba, fig.cap="ESBL/CARBA: National observed vs fitted incidence trajectories with ITS counterfactual. Solid black: observed. Solid blue: fitted ITS. Dashed orange: counterfactual with no interruptions (continuation of pre-2020 trend). Vertical lines mark 2020 and 2022.", out.width="100%"}
f <- "amr_its_plots/its_observed_fitted_counterfactual_esbl_carba.png"
if (file.exists(f)) knitr::include_graphics(f)
```

```{r its-counterfactual-vre, fig.cap="VRE: National observed vs fitted incidence trajectories with ITS counterfactual. Solid black: observed. Solid blue: fitted ITS. Dashed orange: counterfactual with no interruptions (continuation of pre-2020 trend). Vertical lines mark 2020 and 2022.", out.width="100%"}
f <- "amr_its_plots/its_observed_fitted_counterfactual_vre.png"
if (file.exists(f)) knitr::include_graphics(f)
```

```{r its-counterfactual-mrsa, fig.cap="MRSA: National observed vs fitted incidence trajectories with ITS counterfactual. Solid black: observed. Solid blue: fitted ITS. Dashed orange: counterfactual with no interruptions (continuation of pre-2020 trend). Vertical lines mark 2020 and 2022.", out.width="100%"}
f <- "amr_its_plots/its_observed_fitted_counterfactual_mrsa.png"
if (file.exists(f)) knitr::include_graphics(f)
```

### Interpretation of Results

```{r interpretation, results='asis'}
# nolint start
# Helper to pull one row and format IRR + CI + p
get_row <- function(pathogen_name, comparison_name) {
  all_irr %>%
    filter(pathogen == pathogen_name, comparison == comparison_name) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    mutate(
      irr_txt = sprintf("%.2f", IRR),
      ci_txt = sprintf("(%.2f–%.2f)", IRR_lower, IRR_upper),
      p_txt = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value),
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
      ),
      pct_txt = sprintf("%.0f", (IRR - 1) * 100)
    )
}

esbl_covid <- get_row("ESBL/CARBA", "COVID vs Pre-COVID")
esbl_post_pre <- get_row("ESBL/CARBA", "Post-COVID vs Pre-COVID")
esbl_post_covid <- get_row("ESBL/CARBA", "Post-COVID vs COVID")

vre_covid <- get_row("VRE", "COVID vs Pre-COVID")
vre_post_pre <- get_row("VRE", "Post-COVID vs Pre-COVID")
vre_post_covid <- get_row("VRE", "Post-COVID vs COVID")

mrsa_covid <- get_row("MRSA", "COVID vs Pre-COVID")
mrsa_post_pre <- get_row("MRSA", "Post-COVID vs Pre-COVID")
mrsa_post_covid <- get_row("MRSA", "Post-COVID vs COVID")
# nolint end
```

**ESBL/CARBA (period model):** Incidence during the COVID period was not significantly different from pre-COVID (IRR = `r esbl_covid$irr_txt` `r esbl_covid$ci_txt`, p `r esbl_covid$p_txt`). However, the post-COVID period showed a substantial increase compared with both the COVID period (IRR = `r esbl_post_covid$irr_txt` `r esbl_post_covid$ci_txt`, p `r esbl_post_covid$p_txt`; ~`r esbl_post_covid$pct_txt`% higher than COVID) and pre-COVID levels (IRR = `r esbl_post_pre$irr_txt` `r esbl_post_pre$ci_txt`, p `r esbl_post_pre$p_txt`; ~`r sprintf("%.0f", (esbl_post_pre$IRR - 1) * 100)`% higher than pre-COVID). This pattern suggests a delayed surge in ESBL/CARBA infections following the pandemic period. The ITS model indicates an immediate step decrease at 2020 followed by a significant step increase at 2022, consistent with the period model findings and visualized in the counterfactual trajectories.

**VRE (period model):** We observed a pronounced reduction during COVID versus pre-COVID (IRR = `r vre_covid$irr_txt` `r vre_covid$ci_txt`, p `r vre_covid$p_txt`). Post-COVID incidence rebounded strongly relative to the COVID period (IRR = `r vre_post_covid$irr_txt` `r vre_post_covid$ci_txt`, p `r vre_post_covid$p_txt`; ~`r vre_post_covid$pct_txt`% higher than COVID), but was not significantly different from pre-COVID levels (IRR = `r vre_post_pre$irr_txt` `r vre_post_pre$ci_txt`, p `r vre_post_pre$p_txt`). This pattern suggests substantial disruption during the pandemic period followed by recovery to approximately pre-pandemic baseline. The ITS model shows a large immediate step decrease at 2020, consistent with the period model findings, and is visualized in the counterfactual trajectories.

**MRSA (period model):** COVID-period incidence was substantially lower than pre-COVID (IRR = `r mrsa_covid$irr_txt` `r mrsa_covid$ci_txt`, p `r mrsa_covid$p_txt`). The post-COVID period showed a modest rebound relative to the COVID period (IRR = `r mrsa_post_covid$irr_txt` `r mrsa_post_covid$ci_txt`, p `r mrsa_post_covid$p_txt`; ~`r mrsa_post_covid$pct_txt`% higher than COVID), but incidence remained below pre-COVID levels (IRR = `r mrsa_post_pre$irr_txt` `r mrsa_post_pre$ci_txt`, p `r mrsa_post_pre$p_txt`; ~`r sprintf("%.0f", (mrsa_post_pre$IRR - 1) * 100)`% lower than pre-COVID). This pattern suggests partial recovery but not a return to pre-pandemic baseline. The ITS model shows an immediate step decrease at 2020, but no significant step or slope changes at 2022, indicating a gradual recovery that remains below the counterfactual trajectory (see counterfactual plots).

## Geographic Distribution

Figure 1 shows the regional distribution of mean incidence rates for each pathogen across the three time periods.

```{r map-figure, fig.cap="Mean regional incidence of AMR-associated infections in Sweden by COVID period. Left panels show choropleth maps for ESBL/CARBA (top), VRE (middle), and MRSA (bottom) across Pre-COVID (2015-2019), COVID (2020-2021), and Post-COVID (2022-2025) periods. Right panel shows a reference map of Swedish counties.", out.width="100%"}
# Include the pre-generated map
if (file.exists("map_amr_infections_combined.png")) {
  knitr::include_graphics("map_amr_infections_combined.png")
}
```

# Discussion

This analysis reveals substantial disruptions to AMR-associated infection patterns in Sweden during and following the COVID-19 pandemic. The three pathogens showed varying patterns during 2020-2021, likely reflecting:

1. **Changes in healthcare utilization:** Reduced non-urgent healthcare visits may have affected detection and reporting of AMR infections
2. **Antimicrobial use patterns:** Pandemic-related changes in antibiotic prescribing and hospital infection control practices
3. **Travel restrictions:** Reduced international travel may have affected importation of resistant strains, particularly for ESBL/CARBA
4. **Healthcare-associated transmission:** Changes in hospital occupancy and infection prevention measures may have influenced MRSA transmission

The post-COVID recovery patterns differ between pathogens, suggesting pathogen-specific transmission dynamics and varying impacts of pandemic-related disruptions on different AMR-associated infections.

If surveillance artifacts were the sole explanation for the pandemic-era changes, broadly similar patterns would be expected across pathogens; the observed heterogeneity is consistent with pathogen-specific transmission dynamics in addition to surveillance and behavior changes.

## Limitations

- Ecological study design precludes individual-level inference
- Potential surveillance artifacts during the pandemic period
- 2025 population denominators were derived from the latest available SCB monthly population table at the time of analysis (not a finalized annual total)
- Within-county temporal dependence is addressed via cluster-robust standard errors rather than explicit autocorrelation structures (e.g., AR terms); ITS level and slope terms partially capture structured time dependence, but residual autocorrelation may remain
- AMR surveillance may be influenced by testing practices and laboratory capacity, which may have varied during the pandemic

# Conclusion

The COVID-19 pandemic significantly disrupted the epidemiology of AMR-associated infections in Sweden. While incidence rates showed varying patterns during the pandemic period, recovery patterns in 2022-2025 suggest differential impacts on transmission pathways and surveillance. Continued surveillance will be essential to determine whether pre-pandemic patterns will be fully restored and to monitor the long-term impact of pandemic-related changes on AMR epidemiology.

# References

Swedish Public Health Agency. Disease Statistics (Sjukdomsstatistik). Available at: https://www.folkhalsomyndigheten.se/

\newpage

# Appendix: Regional Incidence Data

```{r appendix-table}
# Regional summary
regional_summary <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(region, pathogen, period) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_inc = round(mean(incidence, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = c(pathogen, period),
    values_from = mean_inc,
    names_glue = "{pathogen}_{period}"
  )

regional_summary %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Region = region,
    starts_with("ESBL/CARBA"),
    starts_with("VRE"),
    starts_with("MRSA")
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Mean incidence rates (per 100,000) by region, pathogen, and period",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", rep("r", 9)),
    col.names = c("Region", 
                  "Pre", "COVID", "Post",
                  "Pre", "COVID", "Post",
                  "Pre", "COVID", "Post")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size = 8
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "ESBL/CARBA" = 3, "VRE" = 3, "MRSA" = 3))
```

```{r session-info, include=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
